rationale.claude: Almost target quality on substance — BTB bullets are tight, JD scope is right (Tier 1-2, MFA, Microsoft 365), and the 1-800-GOT-JUNK section is appropriately compressed. But the summary is BLATANTLY doubled: the first sentence ends '...delivering supported experience in user account support, onboarding workflows, and access-related troubleshooting, Microsoft 365, MacOS support.' and is immediately followed by a SECOND complete summary paragraph starting 'IT Help Desk Specialist with 3 years...'. Two summaries got concatenated. The skills section has the same affliction — duplicates everywhere ('MFA' AND 'Multi-factor Authentication', 'Ticketing Systems' AND 'ticketing', 'onboarding workflows' as a multi-word skill embedded in the technical list). These are polish/QA failures on a substantively good draft.

key_strengths: BTB bullet 1 ('Administered Linux Photon servers and NUC kiosks, resolving hardware, OS, and network layer incidents under time pressure') is well-scoped and metric-adjacent
1-800-GOT-JUNK section is appropriately compressed to 6 bullets with the 15% lost-job metric preserved
Date format is canonical ('Sep 2024 – Mar 2026') — no Sept/September drift
AI Job-Application Pipeline project description is concrete and shows architectural thinking ('conveyor belt architecture')
key_weaknesses: Summary is doubled — two complete summary paragraphs run back to back, reading as if a generation step ran twice and both outputs got concatenated

Skills technical list has same item in multiple casings ('MFA' + 'Multi-factor Authentication (MFA)' + 'multi-factor authentication'; 'Ticketing Systems' + 'ticketing'; 'Onboarding Workflows' + 'onboarding')
A SKILL slot contains the phrase 'user account support, onboarding workflows, and access-related troubleshooting' — that's a comma-separated list collapsed into one cell, not a skill
BEAT THE BOMB bullet 7 ('Reduced guest transition wait time 75% (10 min to 2.5 min)') uses a different punctuation style than the rest of the document and lacks a verb-led opening
Soft skills section uses 'Briefing & Status Reporting' which reads as military-specific jargon for a civilian help-desk audience
specific_fixes: Pick ONE summary paragraph and delete the other — the second (IT Help Desk Specialist with 3 years...) is the better choice, drop the first
Dedupe the skills.technical list — keep canonical name only ('Multi-factor Authentication (MFA)' wins, drop 'MFA' and 'multi-factor authentication')
Drop the 'user account support, onboarding workflows, and access-related troubleshooting' skill entry — those are already covered by 'User Account Support' and 'Onboarding Workflows' individually
Rewrite the 75% bullet: 'Reduced guest transition wait time by 75%, from approximately 10 minutes to 2.5 minutes, by improving workflow and handoff processes.'
Replace 'Briefing & Status Reporting' with 'Status Communication' for the civilian context
tier_definition_match: Matches nearmiss (~8): almost submit-ready, polish gaps only. The substantive content is right — JD scope, metrics, structure all good. What's blocking it from 9+ is purely cleanup work (the doubled summary, the duplicate skills) — issues a careful editor can fix in 10 minutes without changing any decision about WHAT the resume says.
